greeting.text: Hi, I'm Aillie, PropSight's property assistant. I can value any HDB or condo on real recent sales, work out your budget, or point you to the right tool. What are you looking to do?

about.label: What is PropSight?
about.text: PropSight's a free Singapore property site that does the homework for you. Value any HDB or condo against real recent sales, work out your budget, stamp duty and grants, read up on any town, and follow the market and the news, all in one place. I'm Aillie, the front desk, so ask me anything and I'll take you straight to the right tool.

free.label: Is everything free?
free.text: Yes, all of it. Value your home against recent sales, check how much you can borrow, work out stamp duty, ABSD and grants, see if you're eligible for a flat, and read the area guides, monthly market analysis and daily property news. Tap a tool or just ask me and I'll take you there.

data.label: Where do your numbers come from?
data.text: Everything's built on official URA and HDB data and real transaction records. The valuation reads recent caveats for your exact town and flat type, and every calculator uses the current official rates. So what I tell you traces back to real sales and real rules, not guesswork.

market.label: What's the market doing?
market.text: Right now the mass market's leading. The suburbs and city fringe are rising and central HDB towns are up sharply, while prime is the one segment slipping. Private homes average about 1,753 dollars psf, up 3.8 percent over the year, and HDB resale's holding near 605 psf. The strongest town is Marine Parade, up 11 percent. So if you're buying, the heartland has the momentum, and prime's where value's starting to turn up.

deepdive.label: The million-dollar HDB paradox
deepdive.text: Here's the paradox everyone's asking about. HDB prices have actually fallen two quarters in a row, the first back-to-back drop in nearly seven years, yet a record 902 flats crossed a million dollars in the first half of 2026. Both are true because they're different flats. The million-dollar ones are bigger, high-floor, long-lease homes in a few central towns like Toa Payoh, Bukit Merah and Queenstown. So if you're buying in the heartland, that record market simply isn't yours, and the typical flat has stopped getting more expensive, so there's less reason to rush than the headlines suggest.
